content.question: Does school closure in Hungarian villages cause measurable population loss beyond what would have occurred due to underlying demographic and economic trends, and if so, does the effect vary by village type, ethnicity composition, or distance to urban centers?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a Hungarian difference-in-differences or regression discontinuity analysis using municipal-level population data (2000-2024) and school closure records, controlling for pre-existing demographic trends and urban proximity, with stratified analysis by Roma/non-Roma enrollment composition.
